target.title: fix(models): bound /models and models list catalog loading
target.body:
## Summary
- Cache shared `/models` provider data so Telegram and Discord reuse the same in-flight build instead of repeating the expensive browse/auth work for every callback.
- Keep the earlier bounded browse catalog loading and auth-discovery reduction in the same PR.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-models.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker.test.ts`
- `openclaw models list --provider litellm --json` on the Homebrew-installed binary

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: Telegram `/models`, provider selection, and model selection are measurably faster after the shared `/models` cache patch and the Homebrew hotfix that was loaded after the restart.
- Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw setup on macOS with the Homebrew-installed `openclaw` binary; gateway was restarted at `13:12:37` before the faster run.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: In Telegram, run `/models`, choose a provider, then choose a model. Compare the before/after screenshots from the same Telegram flow.
- Evidence after fix: Screenshot `截圖 2026-06-12 14.09.05.png` shows `00:20.16` total for the three Telegram stages after the fix. Screenshot `截圖 2026-06-12 03.44.05.png` shows `00:44.10` total before the fix.
- Observed result after fix: The same Telegram path now completes in about half the time, with `/models`, provider selection, and model selection all responding noticeably faster after restart.
- What was not tested: Cross-platform timing reruns on Windows x64 and Linux arm64 in this proof step.

Fixes #91809
